summary: A legendary Europe ’72 Beat‑Club set bristling with volcanic jams (Truckin’, The Other One, Playin’) but hampered by a slow, hissy soundboard source.
review: The performance captures the peak of the spring‑1972 tour – blistering leads from Jerry, a monster 23‑minute The Other One and an incendiary Truckin’/Drums/Other One sequence that many fans rank among the year’s best.  Technically the recording is a degraded SBD with pitch‑slowdown, hiss and occasional feedback, so while the music shines, the audio fidelity remains only fair.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rave about the musical intensity (show rating ~4.2) but consistently criticize the slow‑pitch, hissy soundboard (recording rating ~2.5). Weighted average yields a solid 3.7.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Degraded SBD with noticeable pitch‑slowdown, hiss, and occasional feedback (especially during Loser). The mix is muddy but the jams still cut through; no audience ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A full‑length TV broadcast set (≈90 min) with warm‑up tracks, a tight first half and a powerhouse second half highlighted by Truckin'>Drums>Other One.  Occasional false starts (Sugaree, PITB) add quirks but the overall arc stays energetic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solos are repeatedly praised – intense leads on The Other One and a wildfire solo on Truckin’.  Pitch slowdown is noted but doesn’t dull his brilliance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Black‑Throated Wind is called slower than usual yet unique; overall keyboard work isn’t singled out beyond that.
